perturbation.original_text: In consideration of the services to be rendered by Maimon hereunder and for Maimon's service as a director on the Board of Directors of the Company, the Company will pay Maimon a monthly fee of $1,000 during the Minimum Period, beginning on the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of the services to be rendered by Maimon hereunder and for Maimon's service as a director on the Board of Directors of the Company, the Company will pay Maimon a fee during the Minimum Period, beginning on the Effective Date.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific monthly fee amount, the contract becomes ambiguous regarding the exact compensation. While it states that Maimon will be paid, the absence of the amount creates uncertainty and could lead to disputes. This is an in-text contradiction because, while the contract mentions compensation, the details are now missing, which would be considered a standard clause to have. This omission directly contradicts the expectation of a clear and defined payment structure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the services to be rendered by Maimon hereunder and for Maimon's service as a director on the Board of Directors of the Company, the Company will pay Maimon a fee during the Minimum Period, beginning on the Effective Date.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a monthly fee of $1,000, providing a clear and defined payment structure. The changed text removes the specific amount, stating only that Maimon will be paid a fee. This omission introduces ambiguity and directly contradicts the expectation of a clear and defined payment structure, as the contradicted text would suggest a defined fee. Although the change and contradicted text are identical, the removal of the specific amount in the changed text introduces ambiguity into the contract as a whole, creating an in-text contradiction, according to the explanation provided.

perturbation.original_text: Maimon agrees during the term of this Agreement not to accept work or enter into a contract or accept an obligation inconsistent or incompatible with Maimon's obligations under this Agreement or with the scope of services to be rendered for the Company. Maimon warrants that to the best of Maimon's knowledge, there is no other contract or duty on Maimon's part now in existence inconsistent with this Agreement. During the term of this Agreement and for a period of two (2) years after expiration or termination for any reason of this Agreement, Maimon agrees not to: (a) compete with the business of the Company, whether individually or through any entity, or to use (or permit the use of) any Confidential Information, directly or indirectly, for the purpose of competing with the business of the Company; or (b) suggest to, induce or persuade any customer, client, vendor, supplier, employee, consultant or agent of the Company to terminate or diminish its relationship with the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Maimon agrees during the term of this Agreement not to accept work or enter into a contract or accept an obligation inconsistent or incompatible with Maimon's obligations under this Agreement or with the scope of services to be rendered for the Company. Maimon warrants that to the best of Maimon's knowledge, there is no other contract or duty on Maimon's part now in existence inconsistent with this Agreement. During the term of this Agreement and for a period of two (2) years after expiration or termination for any reason of this Agreement, Maimon agrees not to: (a) compete with the business of the Company, whether individually or through any entity, or to use (or permit the use of) any Confidential Information, directly or indirectly, for the purpose of competing with the business of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the non-solicitation clause, specifically (b), the agreement becomes silent on whether Maimon can solicit Company's stakeholders post-termination. This omission creates an in-text contradiction because the general intent of Section 8 is to prevent Maimon from undermining the Company's business. However, without the non-solicitation clause, Maimon could actively solicit clients and employees, directly conflicting with the implied protection of the Company's interests. The section starts with not accepting work that is incompatible, but then omits the clause regarding solicitation, which could be seen as being incompatible.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Maimon agrees during the term of this Agreement not to accept work or enter into a contract or accept an obligation inconsistent or incompatible with Maimon's obligations under this Agreement or with the scope of services to be rendered for the Company. Maimon warrants that to the best of Maimon's knowledge, there is no other contract or duty on Maimon's part now in existence inconsistent with this Agreement. During the term of this Agreement and for a period of two (2) years after expiration or termination for any reason of this Agreement, Maimon agrees not to: (a) compete with the business of the Company, whether individually or through any entity, or to use (or permit the use of) any Confidential Information, directly or indirectly, for the purpose of competing with the business of the Company.
perturbation.justification: The omission of the non-solicitation clause (b) in the 'Changed Text' creates a contradiction with the overarching intent expressed in the 'Contradicted Text,' which states that Maimon agrees not to accept work or enter into a contract or accept an obligation inconsistent or incompatible with Maimon's obligations. While the altered text still includes the non-compete clause, removing the explicit prohibition on soliciting clients and employees introduces a scenario where Maimon could potentially undermine the Company's business post-termination by soliciting their stakeholders. This contrasts with the initial agreement that Maimon will not accept work or enter into a contract that is incompatible.

perturbation.original_text: If Maimon should become unable to serve as CEO, or should fail to perform any of the obligations hereunder for any cause including death or disability, always in the sole judgment and decision of the Company, then the Company shall have the right to terminate this agreement on five days prior written notice. Maimon shall have the right to resign at any time upon 30 days prior written notice. The Company may in its discretion and at its option terminate this Agreement at any time after the Minimum Period upon five days prior written notice to Maimon.
perturbation.changed_text: If Maimon should become unable to serve as CEO, or should fail to perform any of the obligations hereunder for any cause including death or disability, always in the sole judgment and decision of the Company, then the Company shall have the right to terminate this agreement on five days prior written notice. Maimon shall have the right to resign at any time upon 30 days prior written notice.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause that gives the Company the right to terminate the agreement after the Minimum Period, the contract introduces an inconsistency. This now implies that after the Minimum Period the Company can't terminate the agreement unless it is related to Maimon being unable to serve as CEO, which then puts Maimon in a much more powerful position and it is a key change that would go against the rest of the agreement. This creates uncertainty in enforcement as it contradicts the expected flexibility of the Company to end the agreement. The contradiction lies in the section mentioning the circumstances for termination, but it excludes the company's discretionary termination right, which is standard practice.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If Maimon should become unable to serve as CEO, or should fail to perform any of the obligations hereunder for any cause including death or disability, always in the sole judgment and decision of the Company, then the Company shall have the right to terminate this agreement on five days prior written notice. Maimon shall have the right to resign at any time upon 30 days prior written notice.
perturbation.justification: The removal of the Company's right to terminate after the Minimum Period directly contradicts the overall intent and implied flexibility of the agreement as described in the explanation. The contradicted text emphasizes the company's termination rights under specific circumstances, while the changed text eliminates the company's discretionary right to terminate after a specified period. This creates an inconsistency regarding the conditions under which the agreement can be terminated, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as described by the Omissions definition, since a key clause was removed that creates conflict within the document.
